title: Algorithmic Scoring System Platform (Beta)
author: bite-the-apple
selftext:
We are building a stock market research platform that allows novice investors and retail traders make insightful investment decision. Our scoring system synthesizes more than 140 fundamental, technical and market sentiment indicators.   
Our Beta is available for free. Let us know If interested to learn more or be part of the team.  
\-- Not financial advice, educational information only --

comments.author: dogazine4570
comments.body:
Interesting concept — there’s definitely demand for tools that help newer investors filter noise. A few questions that might help people evaluate the beta:

- How are the 140+ indicators weighted? Static weights, factor-model based, or adaptive/ML-driven?
- Is the scoring system transparent (can users see which factors drive a stock’s score), or is it more of a black box?
- How do you handle regime shifts? Many technical/sentiment indicators degrade in different volatility environments.
- Have you done any out‑of‑sample backtesting, and if so, how do transaction costs/slippage impact results?

For retail users especially, interpretability and risk context matter as much as the score itself. A high score without volatility, drawdown, or factor exposure info could be misleading.

If you’re sharing access, it’d be helpful to see a sample report or dashboard screenshot so people understand the UX and depth.

Curious to see how you differentiate from existing screeners like Finviz, Koyfin, or QuantConnect-style factor tools.

replies.author: bite-the-apple
replies.body:
Thank you for raising all those points and for your feedback. We are in the pro bring on a quant as a co-founder and are working to optimize exactly those points you have mentioned.  
I'll speak to the team and we might be able to add an Implied Volatility score to the main result page to complement the algorithm score.  
Accessibility is our main difference when it comes to difference with platforms like Finviz, Koyfin, Factset, Ycharts, etc. is accessibility (price and mobile app), intuitive UI design and clear presentation of important information so that it is easy to navigate for both novice investors and retail traders.  
Check us out [Verex Markets](https://verexmarkets.com). Would love to hear what you think. We will reveal more features from our final product periodically as we approach launch.
